sections.heading: Set Documents to Skip
sections.body: To specify the number of documents to skip:

sections.heading: Step 1: Specify the number of documents to skip.
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

- In the Query Bar, click **Options**.

- Enter an integer representing the number of documents to skip
  into the **Skip** field.

  ![Results of using the Skip option](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/querybar/query-skip-success.png)

- Click Find to run the query and view the updated
  results.

  > **Note:** 
  >
  > For query result sets larger than 1000 documents, Atlas shows a
  > subset of the results.  Otherwise, Atlas shows the entire result
  > set.
  >
  > For details on sampling, see [sampling](atlas-ui-sampling).

sections.heading: Clear the Query
sections.body:
To clear the query bar and the results of the query, click
Reset.

sections.heading: How Does the Atlas Query Compare to MongoDB and SQL Queries?
sections.body:
[$skip](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/skip) corresponds to the `LIMIT ... OFFSET ...` clause
in a SQL (Structured Query Language) `SELECT` statement.

> **Example:** You have a 3,235 articles. You would like to see a list of articles
> grouped in blocks of 50, starting with the 436th record.
>
> SQL
>
> ```sql
> SELECT * FROM article
> LIMIT 50 OFFSET 435;
> ```
>
> MongoDB Aggregation
>
> ```javascript
> db.article.aggregate(
>   { $limit : 50 },
>   { $skip : 435 }
> );
> ```
>
> Atlas
>
>  Skip Option
>
> ```javascript
> $skip : 435
> ```
